subject: Physics
title: Quantum Tunneling
description: A quick win: understand Quantum Tunneling.

hook.text: A particle hits a wall it doesn't have enough energy to cross—and appears on the other side anyway.

buildup.text: In classical physics, a ball rolling toward a hill too tall to climb just bounces back. Always.

discovery.text: Quantum particles have a wave-like probability spread. Part of that wave leaks through the barrier.

twist.text: The Sun shines because of tunneling. Hydrogen nuclei tunnel through their repulsion to fuse.

climax.text: Flash drives, transistors, and radioactive decay all depend on quantum tunneling.

punchline.text: A particle walks through walls. Not magic—quantum mechanics.

quiz.question: What is quantum tunneling?
options: A particle passing through a barrier it shouldn't have enough energy to cross
Electrons jumping between atoms in a wire
